reviews.review_title: Great game! Exactly what I hoped it woul
reviews.review_description: I even played it start to finish. I loved it! It was exactly what I was looking for in a VR game! Honestly, I seldom finish a VR game. Many are fun at first but become either tedious, repetitive, or bogged down in overly complex systems. Your game had no such issues. I kept wanting to keep pushing forward to see what was next!
Add more to the campaign keep up the good work looking forward to a sequel to this or DLC's or more levels more gruesome zombies her protest limbs silver serving
One of the best games on the system, really a hidden gem

There are many zombie shooters in VR and most of them focus on the waves of undead and mowing them down. 

The Living Remain is NOT one of these. 

This game focuses on survival, use of environment, and has a full storyline it walks you through. Progress is pretty linear but requires and rewards exploration.

I left out a star due to some very clear invisible walls which was disconcerting, and a little bit of immersion-breaking ability to manipulate some items in-world, but not more. 

Keep this one coming!
One of the best  vr games for sure

reviews.review_title: A perfect (but too short) zombie game
reviews.review_description: Survival horror fans will love this one. It's a classic tale of survival and zombies. You'll be making your way throughout an impressively detailed city in hopes of escaping and finding your family. The plot is pretty standard, and the ending cutscene feels a little rushed, but gameplay is where this game shines. Guns are great to use, and there's a good variety to find. The upgrade system is a nice touch, too. You'll be able to max out your favorites. There are some pretty tense moments that even had me panicking a little. And any time the game starts to feel repetitive, it throws a new mechanic at you to keep things fresh. The only drawside is that once you complete the game, there isn't any replay incentive other than some collectibles and additional difficulties (I beat my first playthrough on the hardest difficulty with minimal issue, so VR/Survival Horror veterans should just start on Hard anyway). Overall, I highly recommend this one for anyone who loves the genre.

If you want to play a really good zombie shooter, then play Arizona Sunshine 1, 2, Remake, or Paradise Hotel. And if you have already finished those games and are still bored, then just replay them again! That would still be better than playing The Living Remain! :)

However, because The Living Remain tries to imitate Arizona and Paradise Hotel, I still cannot call it a bad game. No, it is not a bad game — it is just worse even than Arizona Sunshine 1, which came out 10 years ago!

I heard this VR game was made by just two people, but I absolutely fell in love with it as a zombie VR game.
Part of it is that I've always loved zombies. My favorite zombies are the ones created by director George A. Romero, and this kind of VR zombie game made my dream of immersing myself in that world come true.
And among the several zombie VR games out there, this one perfectly matched my ideal image of zombies. It made me feel like I was the protagonist in one of Romero's zombie movies.
It's great that the game features several fields, including cityscapes. The atmosphere of these fields, filled with the sense of people's daily lives yet overrun by zombies, really excites me.
Honestly, I wish someone with money would fund these two developers to create a zombie VR game with even more possibilities. If they had sufficient funding, I'm sure they could make an even more amazing VR game.
Right now, I've cleared the game once and am playing through it again.
Because it was just that much fun.

What happens if you take George Romero's day of the dead and throw it in a blender with Arizona Sunshine? you get this unpolished gem of a game. After a brief tutorial you're left to figure out how to progress (though the game is linear in design) and take on the undead, with story beats told through a walkie talkie

I loved my time with this; the atmosphere is creepy and foreboding with a great ambient soundtrack. Graphically it's in the "good enough" department, but what this lacks visually, it makes up for in gameplay.

My only complaint is how useless the knife seems to be? 

Recommend, especially if it's on sale
Everything in this game is confusing to navigate

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving the game a try. We understand that not every style will click for everyone. The game uses VR-focused interactions and exploration-based progression, which some players really enjoy once they get familiar with the mechanics. Are there any specifics we can help you with? We can always walk you through it. If you'd like,  you can email us at support@fivefingerstudios.com or via our Discord channel.

Five Finger Studios

reviews.review_description:
I played a lot of zombies, and this one is very good for a campaign focused zombie game. 

I get spooked very easily and this game isn't too much on the scary side (I take time progressing very slowly and check every corner tho) 

The sound design is decent. I can hear zombies grunting and footsteps coming at me, so I don't get jump scares out of nowhere.

What surprised me the most is hearing that the devs behind this are a team of 2 people, wife and husband just doing what they loved together and I can feel the passion behind it simply by just playing the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you, this means so much to us because we really poured everything we had into our game!

reviews.review_description: There's a few rough edges that still need to be polished, but overall its a really good game (especially considering its a team of just two). If you like Zombie games, then what are you waiting for!
Would love more gameplay maybe second game or a dlc letting you keep your previous weapons
Honestly the only bad thing is that I finished it in one sitting lol.  Absolutely a blast playing it and had a fun time surviving.
Super fun game and great campaign but it’s a little too short. The graphics are great and the guns are incredible. Overall definitely worth the money.

I am enjoying playing through, although i did experience a bug where i was at the part where you cant go down the stairs without a flashlight, with him saying the lines. Which i already picked up from the drawer and just won't proceed any further. Restarting the game sorted it. But nice job still

This is a genuinely good game. Sure, it doesn’t have cutting-edge graphics, there are a few physics and weapon-handling glitches, and it’s shorter than you’d hope — but it’s very well designed, with great atmosphere, interactive environments, solid enough physics, variety, and some really clever gameplay ideas. All the more impressive that it was made by just two people. I finished it and thoroughly enjoyed it — highly recommended, a true hidden gem.
I'm not a big fan of zombie games but I really like this one, it's very atmospheric. Great ragdoll physics, dunno why but it gives me half life vibes
A solid zombie VR adventure, fun and atmospheric. I would recommend it, and it was made by two people, they should be pleased with what they’ve produced, I look forward to seeing more.

Really enjoyable game. It’s not a masterpiece, but there’s something about it that keeps me coming back.
If you’ve played Arizona Sunshine and you’ve been missing that kind of experience, this one will hit the spot.

So I recently got the game at the low price it currently has. I really love zombie games so I thought why not. I read the reviews and most are about clunky mechanics with weapons so I went ahead and got it anyways because people sometimes overreact sometimes. What one loves another doesn't right? So into the game I went! Now level design is really enjoyable. And graphics are also ok, I like it so far.
Zombies are not to bad and I can see they share the same ones from another game I recently played even down to the running ones who share the same motions so that was a bit of a bummer. The voice acting of the main protagonist is somewhat meh. However the voice of the sidekick via radio is nice (I remember that voice think it's some actress?) 

And now the bad part... The weapon control's are bad as in frustratingly BAD. for instance, placement of the weapons can only be adjusted height wise and not width wise. Me being a big guy of 1.91m the gun belt looks like a miniskirt. When you shoot zombies aiming is really a hit or mis. Even if you do the exact same thing. Sometimes you can shoot a zombie up close in the head and sometimes it hits nothing... Reloading is bad and I do mean BAD. Grabbing a magazine or clip from over your shoulder Won't work half of the time. When you are in a heavy fight with lots of zombies and you can't reload due to bad mechanics is super frustrating! I also found out you can take a shotgun and put it over your right shoulder but good luck getting it when you need it. I once stood there reaching to get my shotgun for 30 times yes 30 before I got it to work. Same deal with putting it back.

I feel the devs should've developed the weapons mechanics alot better. Especially reloading... Because if they would've done it, this game would be way better for it. 

Me I'm not going to refund the game because I want them to make more games but guys pls let it cook some more next time and I hope you can give this one an update? If it gets fixed I'll change to 5 ⭐
It’s just another zombie game, but it’s done pretty well and a lot of fun to play through. Gets intense and definitely action packed. Good job 👏

Guns are pretty well established in VR so there’s not really any excuse for the way these ones operate, and you either kill the zombie or get virtually no feedback which is is why it looks that way in the video. The gunplay isn’t great in this game.  

The game is built like Arizona Sunshine but the vibe I keep getting is Paradise Hotel, which is good but it isn’t Paradise Hotel. 

This game needs technical polish to be fun.

reviews.review_title: we’ VR family i got my family with me.
reviews.review_description:
A good game. A work of love. 
Wonderful thing that the present VR economy can give up indie title that can compete with much more financed game.
With a fistful of GB , VR games can blast  our mind. Conventional game console cannot do that….well done FIVE FINGERS, your story is so refreshing and positive. I am happy to contribute to your success. This is a good game. This is solid fun. It makes you like a lean efficient machine. Well thought out.
A very pleasant experience.

I bought this on a whim as there are only so many story driven shooters on the Quest system, and I was not disappointed. It really plays very well for such a small development team. If you enjoy a good fps, then give it a go. It really is excellent!

I'm impressed by what a 2-man team can accomplish. This game is inspired by old school Resident Evil games and Arizona Sunshine, which have a varied environments / stages, and unique puzzles to solve in order to progress. The atmosphere is great, the background music keeps you on edge,  there's also wind and water effects in-game. When a horde of zombies attack they can be quite challenging, but you can adjust the difficulty accordingly. The story is easy to follow and mostly linear, but there are scattered hidden items to find. Overall, if you're looking for another zombie game to play, this is one to add to the list. 👍
Easily one of the most fun experiences I have had on my Quest 3.  The game just starts instantly with action, and never lets up.

Title is a tiny bit lame as I don't consider it a clone, but a faithful adaptation of the "world is infested with zombies" linear story game.
Switch zombies with monsters and lies beneath and metro fall in the same category.
That said, if you know and or like Arizona sunshine, ya this is almost the same. And i recommend to get it asap, especially when it's on sale(got it for 7, 8ish euro
Bit less bling compared to the second and a bit more refined then the first so that is why i went for the 1.5.
I call this a very good game for the money.
Good for the price. No complaints. Not as polished as Arizona but still fun. Good water effects in the sewer level

Quickly becoming my favorite VR game. The storyline is interesting, and the physics and gameplay are amazing. Little things like being able to do a partial reload on a revolver. You can tell the developers put a lot of time and effort into the puzzles, gameplay, and progression of the levels. I'm about halfway through right now (I think) and struggle to put the headset down. I feel like I'm playing Cry of Fear except in VR! It's freaking awesome!!! Kudos to the developers. The physics are smooth as glass and very satisfying. This is a masterpiece of a game!

reviews.review_title: This zombie game has a very good。
reviews.review_description: This zombie game has a very good atmosphere in the room, but the outdoor environment is a bit weak. Overall, it is still very good. The indoor environment has a very scary feeling, and the overall performance is very good. Keep it up

Don't expect anything visually stunning or smooth interactions!
 saying that for a zombie game this game just does it for me really really enjoyed playing this deffo recomended!! You can tell alot of work has gone into it
Just a simple multi-hour zombie campaign. I really liked it though I did get stuck at a couple spots.  I look forward to trying whatever this team puts out next. Well done!

I have finally finished playing the game and I must say that I was very impressed. Just to let you know that I have played pretty much all of the zombie games available on Meta  Quest including all the Arizona Sunshine games, Drop Dead, The Cabin, Zombieland, After the Fall and I am happy to have added this game to the collection.
The game play (story) is very captivating and you are always tempted to continue after each completed segment. The feel of the game is awesome as you can  move around quite smoothly and you can use your hands  to move around objects or open doors or containers besides the obvious shooting..You will have to get used to reloading your weapons and staying calm because when the zombies come ate you the panic sets in and you can easily fumble around and lose everything from your hands.
The choice of weapons are great but you have to earn them after completing numerous rounds. Don't forget to check every corner or behind boxes for ammo and filament which you will need to upgrade your arsenal.
I was very pleased by the graphics, atmosphere and surroundings...The sounds of the zombies are there ! There was a part in the game that you are in the sewers and you have to wade through the water ( even chest high)..The splash sound was there..You even have to swim under the water at one point.
 
In conclusion this is a must have for Zombie fans out there...You wont regret it. Don't listen to the negative comments out there. Thumbs up to the developers of this game..You will have to come up with another zombie sequel for sure !

This is the first zombie game I have played and I thought it was great!

I liked the knife it felt really satisfying digging the knife into the heads of the zombies and I liked the guns and how you upgraded them and I liked the level design and the atmosphere.

The graphics are not that great. I laughed when I saw that the trash was a jpeg image and some of the buttons on the machines are flat jpegs as well. But it doesn't take away from the game because it is still a good game. Also the sounds some of the zombies made when you killed them was a bit silly but still doesn't take away from the game.

The story narrative was a bit too simple and basic from what I was expecting. The overall campaign is great but the story was a bit too simple. Would love a more fleshed out story in future games.

Overall it was a great game! I look forward to a sequel if the developers have that in mind.

Updated Review…

Originally I basically stated that LR is an underling, although also great, to Arizona Sunshine.

I was wrong. LR is different. It is less polished in a narrative sense, but it has some gritty differences I love! The physics and world object interactions really make this more immmersive.upgrading weapons is another plus…

Most importantly, I can’t stop playing it! Different but amazing in its own right - this is your next franchise zombie game.

2 devs. Wow - imagine what they could do with a larger team and more investment. Buy the game and support a clearly talented duo.

Good story that keeps you coming back. Just finished the game and had lots of fun. Nice mechanics and puzzled paths that are welcome. 
Wish there will be co-op mode in the future so I could come back to it.

reviews.review_title: A ZOMBIE GAME TO RIVAL THE BEST
reviews.review_description:
I was lucky enough to get an early copy of this game & previous reviews on other platforms were not wrong, this game is just outstanding, possibly not as good in the graphic department as Saints & Sinners or Arizona Sunshine but they really fit well in this game.

The story is so engaging, you just don’t want to stop playing. I’m 75% in and am really having a lot of fun.
Gun mechanics are spot on, also it’s great fabricating upgrade and finding the lost toys is adds to the experience.

I can’t thank the devs enough for bringing this to Quest, it was on my wishlist as soon as I .saw the trailer.
Bravo Five Finger Studios.

I have been enjoying The Living Remain. Only complaint is that the grip is off a bit. Other issue is that the locked blue cooler in the back yard of the house that gets overrun. I have grabbed the key before and after the house is overrun and it still stays locked. Can you tell me how to pop the lock on that cooler.

developer_response.body: Happy you are enjoying The Living Remain! We responded to your email request for tips to get through this puzzle.

reviews.review_description: So the first couple minutes of this game were pretty bad and I expected I would be submitting for a refund. It has a rudimentary feel and was glitchy. Even getting through the tutorial was an effort at the gun firing part. I did it over and over before I could eventually pass. The everything i touched with my right hand would get stuck to my hand. Even a chair in the office and even though the grip hold setting was off. Luckily, after getting to the first checkpoint everything improved. Picking up objects off the ground isn’t always smooth. But there were a lot of fun elements in the game with the different environments to navigate through in a variety of ways. There was a story. There were a couple of small puzzle-type elementsthat increased my enjoyment   I wasn’t able to swim but I did have a weapon in 1 hand so I don’t know if that affected it. I was playing undead citacel at the same time and found it very boring. I definitely preferred this game and was sad when it was over.
Well I sit playing games and can't reach the gun when sitting it's too low.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! We understand how frustrating it can be when things don’t feel quite right, but everyone’s VR setup can be a bit different. This is not necessarily a bug with the game — in many cases, it can be resolved by adjusting calibration or settings to better match your environment.

We’d love to help you get it working the way it should! You can reach us on our Discord channel at https://discord.com/invite/g7vYGudHF3 or email us directly at support@fivefingerstudios.com so we can walk you through the adjustments.

— Five Finger Studios

reviews.review_title: I jumped the gun a little too early..
reviews.review_description: So far, the game is incredibly average. At times, dipping below that. The graphics are dookie, the melee combat using your knife is an absolute joke, and the story is only interesting if youve never heard the word "zombie" before. But,the biggest issue im having with the game so far, (im about an hour and a half in) is what does it do to stand out from all of the other dozens of zombie games? So far, not a thing, and that really twists my nipps. There's been a drought in good VR games lately so I quickly jumped on this when I saw a few good reviews on it. If you're as jaded on the zombie genre as I am, stay away. It just ain't it, chief.

Buyer be WARNED, this game is more like in Bata testing then a real game so if crappie graphics and physics is your thing,  then this is the game for you...lol...they didn't even get the strike distance ANYWHERE close when it comes to fighting with the knife. In other words, if you go kill with the knife, you have to stap in front of the face in order to hit and yet the zombies can swing at you, CLEARLY MISSING and yet you still get hit. Then when you get to the room where there is a broken ladder that you SOME HOW still have to use and there IS NOTHING TO GRAB!!! So the game is BROKEN HERE!! Nothing to grab, nothing to climb, nothing move. Just a broken part of the ladder on the ground that you can't TOUCH! BELIEVE ME, if I could get a refund, I WOULD,w but apparently, I went a few minutes past the time limit! Don't say I DIDN'T WARN YOU...LOL

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you had a frustrating experience and appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. We understand that things like graphics, physics, and melee mechanics can come down to personal preference, and we're actively working to keep improving on all fronts.

Regarding the knife combat, we’re continuing to fine-tune the hit detection and always welcome feedback to help guide that process.

As for the ladder section — it's actually not a bug, but part of a puzzle designed to encourage exploration. We know it can catch some players off guard, so if you'd like a hint or need help getting through it, feel free to reach out to us directly on Discord or at support@fivefingerstudios.com. We’re here to help and truly appreciate everyone giving The Living Remain a shot.

reviews.review_description: Great playable game, is anyone else having trouble collecting the 3D filaments? After the first 3D printer in the ISO container there after the 3D filaments just drop to the floor when placing over the shoulder. Therefore making the game unplayable due to not being able to craft anything

Highly enjoyable. Surprisingly involving.Nothing new by any means, but this is a great old-school corridor shooter that reminds me of classics like Half-Life 2 more than it does Arizona Sunshine( Though if you like AZ, you'll probably enjoy this). The atmosphere is more Dawn Of The Dead than Walking Dead and the music and sound further cement that feeling. The graphics, whilst not flashy are clear, atmospheric and have really good depth: The general feeling of immersion is spot on. Gun-play is fun and fluid, melee is simpler than it's peers but fits well with the general direction of the game. Climbing is actually ENJOYABLE! The story isn't anything mind-blowing, but it's well written and  as a package everything coheres so nicely. I think that for a 2 man Dev team this is a fantastic piece of work. It seems they set out to make a quality, fun and involving classic zombie shooter experience, with masterful level design that skillfully shepherds you through it's environment in a way that isn't generally done anymore. In fact much of the game design here is so considered and thoughtful that I decided against knocking a star off for the occasional stutter because that would be peevish. The Living Remain is fun, absorbing, immersive  and superb value for money. It's also obviously made by people who love this project like it is their actual child. It really shows. With indie micro-team games like this, Metacity patrol, The Infinite Inside, By Grit Alone and Stellar Plans available on the Quest, I couldn't really care less if AAA companies never make another V.R game. This is what your Quest was made for:  Labours of love produced by an indie cottage industry as inspired and absorbing as it was back in the days when games came on cassette tapes.

This could be a decent zombie shooter but the bugs need to be fixed. Enemies walking thru chain link fences… that kinda stuff. 
The biggest gripe I have is the aiming though… the hit box is way off. Times I know I hit the head from 5 ft out, and the bullet misses wide.  
This is a really low budget Arizona sunshine… I’d be happy to play it if these things are fixed. Nothing ruins a shooter quicker than inaccurate aiming.  I’ll update to 5 stars once it’s fixed. I’d also suggest lowering the price to $10-15. $20 seems steep.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to play our game and write a review. We are addressing these issues in our upcoming update, which will be available this weekend. We are always looking to improve The Living Remain, so this feedback helps us do just that.

reviews.review_description:
Feels like a budget Arizona sunshine with a bit more horror elements. Great game but it has three problems I’ve found early on:

1. When playing seated the holsters are still too low even on max height. I’m 6’1 if that helps. There also too inside my body when sitting. 

2. When grabbing items you have to be too accurate and can easily not grab things like drawers your first time.

3. It’s feels impossible to catch a partially empty magazine while ejecting it. Making me need to pick up the magazine off the ground every time with finicky grab mechanics.  It’s really annoying.

Great game overall and remember, aim for the head!

Been longing for a new zombie shooter, excited to get this today. If u liked Arizona sunshine as much as I did 1 and 2 well u will love this game, its also got a metro awaking vibe as well.  This is a super fun fantastic thrill ride with cool vent crawling and walking, its nice to be able to finally stand up in a vent and walk, nice make u think puzzle elements as directions to proceed are cool little mysteries which I dig. Game is brillant and for the money of only 20 bucks might be the best zombie shooter for that price ever. It's a gem 💎

reviews.review_title: Unfinished and glitchy don't buy yet.
reviews.review_description:
This game is infuriating. Movement at a snails pace. Weapons that are hard to aim even at point blank range. Crashing, disappearing pistols (that has happened twice), and everything just feels . . .  untested.
Did they even finish this game? 
Since we have so many zombie games available. AND I BUY THEM ALL. I'll admit. The bar is pretty high.  This is just noise. It just does not shine in any area.

I bought this game the day it became available. I played it for hours each of the next four days and was able to finish it on the easy level.  I LOVED THIS GAME!  It's like you are in a real zombie movie. 

It has a few problems with physics, picking up and holding things, the flashlight when attached to the chest doesn't move up down with the body movement, but other than that, this game has GREAT potential.  Figuring out the puzzles of how to get from one location to the next, finding the lost items and surviving the hordes.  

I got stuck in one area for about a half hour where I could not figure out how to get to the next location, then it suddenly hit me. The answer was right in front of my face the whole time and I just didn't realize it. 

I score this a very strong 8 out of 10 with the belief that after a little more work, it will become a 10. 

Congratulations on making a fun and challenging zombie shooter.

First of I'm enjoying it, enough to keep me playing despite the bugs I came across. 
The game crashed on me at the first lvl transition/loading point and shortly after trying to access the options menu the game shut off audio but never entered the options menu, just continued to run the game. That was the second restart required. Another restart was required when on the 3d printer tutorial, I dropped the filament cable in before being prompted to do so, that totally flummoxed the game, the filament disappeared and I couldn't print the knife attachment, so restart required.
The guns need work, you can  miss at point blank range, as in muzzle almost touching  zombie head. 
The Melee combat is bad , you can't grab the zombie by the head and shank it in the skull as in saints and sinners, and slashing does nothing. 
The holster position definitely needs to be able to raise it a bit further for seated play.
Some graphics anomalies where pieces of items are missing and the picking up of items can be a bit hit or miss, you have to be quite precise in where your hand is when gripping.
But the game play is fun, and so far, the story is engaging enough, and the fact that a 2-person dev team created this is amazing .This is definitely worth the asking price!
I'm looking forward to an update that fixes these few bugs, and I hope there is a Quest 3 update in the future for better graphics.

I can’t grab ammo 

Please fix this game

developer_response.body: This does not seem to be a known bug, please email us at support@fivefingerstudios.com or message us on Discord https://discord.com/invite/g7vYGudHF3 so that we can troubleshoot with you.

reviews.review_description: I get it’s new but there are so many glitches first the shooting is awful no matter how you adjust it, left hand won’t let go of things right hand occasionally gets stuck as well. Can’t pause the game and when you try it just keeps playing and the sounds gets taken away. Graphics are good but it needs some serious work. Refund for sure. Maybe later when it’s been around longer and things are working better but for now I’d wait.

Right from the start you can see that this game is not a high budjed game but what it lacks on production values it makes it up in atmosphere. The sounds are very high quality and you get George A. Romeros Day of the Dead vibes when you are in doors. Otherwise this game feels a bit of mixture of Arizona Sunshine without the comedy style and Dead Horizon without the arcade style.
Sure there are things to improve and i really hope that there will be a lot of updated in the future. 
Shooting could be a bit more accurate when firing at long distance an also when hitting zombie a head that should feel more punchy and one thing this game also fails to me like all seems to do is that I can't stand running zombies at all. They ruin the atmosphere right away. Good thing not all zombies wear their running shoes here but the ones that does i don't like a bit. So that is my most major rant to this game. Remove the runners and I think all mighty Romero would give thumbs up. Very potential 5 star game.

I like it but it does have a few glitches. Like when I go to grab my knife I keep picking up the torch and vice versa. Also could you please add where the belt goes higher as I play all games sat down in a chair with arms and I can't see the belt let alone grab anything from it. I have to keep a weapon in my hands at all times. But that's kind of impossible when I'm required to climb.

developer_response.body: Hey, thank you for taking the time to give us your feedback. We do have holster height adjustment in the option menu. With that said, we plan on increasing the adjustment distance to accommodate seated playing on our next update.

reviews.review_description:
Basically a sequel in style to the original Arizona Sunshine.

Remember how we all bought huge games at much higher prices by much larger teams, and had to complain about constant crashes, annoying pop-ins, non-VR button press interactions, menus instead of holsters, ... Well, this is the opposite. It was made by a tiny and passionate team on a small budget, and it is really moderately priced, yet it has none of these problems.

You can't expect AAA graphics or polish here, but in a VR world filled with silly simulators and rogue-likes, this game stands out as a fully narrated story-driven single player campaign, and I really respect and appreciate that.

In more than one way, this feels like a spiritual successor to the original Arizona Sunshine, with the main character talking to himself and his walkie talkie, the zombie masses charging at and overwhelming you, the horror elements, and the story leading you through different environments and settings. So, if that's what you are looking for, don't think twice about getting The Living Remain.

Graphics are ok, but there needs to be better support for holster placement, and height adjustment for being seated. The shooting seems off. Will update when I have more time to play, and as updates arrive. Don’t let my review hinder you from getting this game. So far it is a fun game to play. Just needs some adjusting. 4 stars. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Game is really cool,devs have done an excellent job with the atmosphere as you go from one location to another,it doesn’t feel like you’re pinned down to one location for too long. it has a decent enough story,it obviously isn’t novel/movie quality but I find it more engaging than,say the Zero Caliber games(great games but story is insanely boring). This game keeps you on your toes,especially in dark areas where you have to depend on your flashlight. I like how they implemented the 3D printer to upgrade your weapons and to make your own ammo. In some areas you might have to dispatch a few stragglers but in other areas the hordes of zombies are quite large. What is the most mind blowing to me,is the fact this game was developed by a married couple. I think that is both awesome and very impressive. There was clearly a lot of love put into this game. Thank you to the devs for bringing this gem to Quest

reviews.review_title: Great Zombie FPS Shooter, couple hiccups
reviews.review_description:
I really am loving this game after finishing Arizona Sunshine 1 & 2. I love the weapon upgrade system using a 3D printer. I've ran into a few glitches here and there but nothing to game-breaking. Coming from Arizona Sunshine as I'm sure many are there's some things you need to be aware of.

1) You can drop and lose your gun. It does not automatically "reholster" like in AS. Listen carefully to hear that it reholstered before hitting a checkpoint as I did restart a few chapters in when I lost my fully upgraded pistol.

2) You will find a pistol again and from what I can tell all applied upgrades somehow automatically apply to the new pistol you find so maybe don't restart when you realize you've dropped a weapon.

3) Grabbing clips/magazines from my shoulder feels less real then a hip reload... wish I could pick where I grab magazines from.

4) You can actually carry 4 weapons! Unless you are at a spot where you need two hands to climb you can have two sidearms, a shoulder weapon, AND a shotgun or rifle in your hands... My current load out is a pistol on right side, SMG on left side, rifle on shoulder, and a shotgun in my hands. You can drop an empty shotgun or rifle and grab your shoulder weapon... when the horde is finished reload everything and move forward again with 4 guns!

5) Get used to having a gun in dominant hand and fighting with your knife in your non-dominant hand... you can run through many areas with sparse zombies dispatched with the knife but have your gun ready for the surprise horde

6) The game doesn't explain this but you can dump all your filament in any 3D printer and the excess you don't use on upgrades will magically be in the next upgrade station you find... that stressed me out for a bit thinking I was losing all un-used filament...

I hope they add a better save system that has QuickSave and Reloads.... as the game sits now you can literally drop all your guns right before a checkpoint and have an unplayable save and that's it. 8/10

reviews.review_title: Doesn't come close to the best
reviews.review_description:
However...it's a decent budget zombie story driven game. It just fell short for me. 
Walking is a bit laboured, impact of kills isn't satisfying. 
Still better than Aliens though.

I’ve ran into a strange bug where either the environment is dimly lit and zombies are graphically lit or environment is brightly lit and zombies are shadow silhouettes until face to face then can be seen. The wall scaling can be buggy and aim is slightly off. That aside the game is fun has tense moments and definitely scratches my fps zombie story driven campaign itch, yes that was a mouthful of brains explanation lol. The price is just right to ignore the game’s shortcomings if this, like me is your type of genre go ahead and purchase you won’t be disappointed.

Set your expectations to just have a few minutes of fun, it is not a AAA title so don't expect it to be... It definitely has potential to improve, be at the same level as Walking Dead & Arizona Sunshine. Devs: Please smooth out the grab glitches, walking pace is too slow, allow storage for healing drinks, and a laser pointer upgrade is a must-have. Looking forward to updates and fixes.

It's 3.5 stars but I am being generous and ignoring most of the bugs.v First off it's very generic. The graphics and gameplay are all pretty standard. But the level design is good. It compares very well to Arizona Sunshine games in single player but because of the price and small download size it's probably a better purchase overall. AS 2 is massive in file size and the graphics are worse than here but do have a lot more style. Unfortunately this is very buggy so be warned! Sometimes climbing doesn’t work. Restart game. Gun stops firing. Restart game. Sound goes. Restart game. Luckily there are regular check points... Hoping it gets fixes and the will be a 5 start recommendation.

Solid linear Zombie Game.
I really enjoyed it. Played it trough in about 4-5 hours, i explored every corner and turned around every box for loot and collectables. Story is ok and i never got bored during my playtrough.
You can upgrade guns on 3D Printer Stations with filament you find. Zombies arent a big thread on "medium" i died 1 time in the whole game. Zombies are all around the area and sometimes you get rushed by groups of them. Maybe ill make another run on "hard"
Grafics is ok but a quest 3 update would be great.
Remember its a 2 Defs game and its not expensive so dont compare to saints and sinners or other AAA titles.

reviews.review_title: There is a bug in the game and others
reviews.review_description:
So when I get to the level with the poisonous gas. I get to a gate and it won’t open because of a sandbag in the game is blocking it. So then I can’t continue in the game.
Also what I think could make the game better is that we could carry an antidote so we use it any time. And I’m right handed and when I need to reload my gun. I need to have my left hand cross my body to grab the ammo on my left shoulder. 
But I still this game is good and has a lot of potential.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for playing our game! That is actually not a bug, it is a puzzle. No spoilers here, but if you want a hint please send us a DM on our Discord https://discord.gg/j3hbFvvq or email support@fivefingerstudios.com

reviews.review_description:
Honestly, from a small dev team, this game has a lot to offer!

If you're a fan of zombies and thrilling moments, then look no further!  You can clearly see the passion the team put in!  Offering elements of action, horror, and some really cool moments, you will have a great time.

This game is superb! The gunplay is fantastic, the graphics and audio are solid (if a little low-budget), and the story is decent. But what really sets this game apart are the weapons upgrades and the atmosphere.

As you navigate the world you'll come across filament spools that you can use to upgrade your gear and craft new items at a 3D printer. The mechanic is superbly executed and is one of the most innovative ways to improve your equipment I've come across in a video game.

The atmosphere is also excellent. Sure, it's a little generic at times and is very linear, but I just love the feel of the game. It's kind of like Arizona Sunshine meets The Walking Dead, but without the stellar graphics.

I really enjoy shooting the zombies in this game. Some shuffle towards you and are easy pickings, while some will charge are will induce a sense of panic as they get closer and closer.

Really excellent game, especially for the price. With a bit more polish this will be a stellar game.

Highly recommended if you enjoy zombie shooters!
Graphics are decent. Gameplay Pretty solid. Overall, I enjoyed it. A patch or two more will really dial it all in. Could have been a little longer. Thumbs up to the devs.

Still amazing that only two peoples can achieved a VR game like that with a better physic than most VR games.

But graphics ,  scenario,  story telling are cheap.
But it's a real game, not only a demo. 
Give them a real budget and they can make a really good game ! 

It's written Inclued rift crossbuy but it doesn't work... I will ask for a refund and buy it on steam. 
That cool to have both Quest and PCVR but if I have to chose I take PC VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing our game, and for taking the time to leave a review. The issue with cross buy has been resolved.

reviews.review_title: I don’t believe two people made this!?!?
reviews.review_description:
Spoiler - I think this game might have been made a while ago and just ported to Quest recently- I had no idea about that in the review below. I just reviewed it like a normal release… This game is freaking awesome..



This is probably one of the top zombie games in VR of all time. I’m not even kidding when I say that…. I have a ton of games… This is better than almost all of them..

It’s a fantastic story…  The voice acting is top notch.. There is a tiny bit of jankiness here and there, especially when aiming… Because I’m a much better shot in other games Iol.

I can’t believe how many zombies you can fit on the screen… really impressive and the zombie models are great… Better than Saints and sinners zombies for sure.. when you’re getting swarmed, you are really getting swarmed… You’re looking at an endless stream of the undead , marching to fantastic environmental audio !  I felt like I was in one of those classic zombie flicks from the 80s!


I would’ve liked to see the protagonist and the mysterious woman in the factory story were fleshed out a little little better- I think she was actually trying to kill him and he’s just a little bit gullible…  it feels like that was a cut you guys deliberately made… I know I’m not gonna be the only one who wonders what the real story was- 

also, don’t set your options before you start the game because it will just revert back to the default options for grip and seated mode and stuff like that when the game starts… The one and only consequential bug I noticed… Wait until you actually start the game to adjust your options. If you’re having trouble climbing or two handing your gun, it’s your grip settings in your options because it actually sets to default on the weird setting.

The sound design is fantastic… The music queues are great. You really know when you’re about to get swarmed… Also, if you’re looking around to try to figure out your next steps and the character goes “hmmm “, that’s the queue that you are very close to where the solution is… Brilliant to add that I would’ve been walking around that factory for days without that audio queue… Lol.


They really made a game here is that is AAA quality… There are so many different interesting environments, and the things that they managed to pull off on a standalone headset are mind blowing.. there is a big explosion towards the end of the game that you’re not gonna believe you’re looking at..

You are a really talented team… Like head and shoulders above lots of people on AAA studio effort… You released a game that didn’t need a ton of work when it came out… It was a finished product and it did not disappoint… I am totally impressed and look forward to the next thing that you guys do! I hope you have a mailing list because I’m gonna go sign up for it so that I don’t ever miss anything you guys make!

One last helpful hint… When you put the filament into the printer, you cannot take it out again again- but don’t worry it will still be in the next printer you encounter!

A great, short game (under 3 hours) with plenty of zombie action, a thrilling atmosphere, and amazing visuals (especially the sewerage system is quite something). A co-op mode would have been great, even if it doesn't make sense with this title. Unfortunately, the game also has its issues: fences/walls sometimes disappear, the game crashes while loading, or doors, drawers, or cabinets aren't immediately accessible and require repeated access. Aiming is really annoying, though, as it's only truly accurate when a zombie is directly in front of you. The shotgun remains my first choice. From a distance, the other weapons skews significantly. Nevertheless, I had a lot of fun and can recommend it to anyone who already knows the Arizona Sunshine games by heart. 
Thank you for this thrilling and exciting game! The nasty rats, in particular, scared me to death several times!

Yes it's fun but there are some visual bugs and
 upgrades that make no sense for a vr game you can't have an accuracy upgrade if I have a red dot on a zombies head and it misses several times is not good game design and I'm tried of these games saying "every bullet counts" when in reality you have over a thousand bullets by the end at least make it so you have more pistol then everything else at the end of the game I was literally just one handing a with a spray and pray ... because you can't use 2 hands with it without it bugging out the knife isn't even a weapon form what I tried it's a fun gimmick but that's all

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share this feedback, especially early on after launch. A lot of what you’re calling out (weapon handling quirks, upgrade balance, and knife usefulness) were valid issues at the time and helped shape the direction of the game.

Now that some time has passed, we’ve made several updates focused on improving weapon behavior, tuning accuracy and upgrades to better match VR expectations, and fixing two-handed handling bugs. We’ve also continued to refine melee interactions and overall combat balance so play-styles feel more intentional rather than “spray and pray.”

If you ever decide to jump back in, we’d genuinely be curious how it feels now compared to your first experience. Either way, we appreciate you giving the game a shot and sharing detailed thoughts, it helped push the game forward.

reviews.review_title: FINALLY an actual quality Zombie title!
reviews.review_description:
I've nit played much of it at all yet, and though it's nit coming off as AAA yet, it truly is knocking on the door of AAA VR from a few years ago. The graphics are well done for a port of a (3? 5?)-year-old game, the controls feel good even if slightly unreliable eith grabbing ammo off of your shoulder at times, and I was happy to see it at least has more story and some kind if small narrative focus  (something I wished games like Arizona Sunshine did better with), even if the lead character's VO isn't particularly engaging/ completely credible (luckily he doesn't sound like ai like others I've heard, nor is he bad or annoying to hear.).
So the game looks good, feels like a slightly watered down Arizona Sunshine (which is still excellent),  and it has a bit of a narrative to follow from what little I've seen, which is always a HUGE thing for me- good story or not.

I'm 5'3", an adult that's obviously not average height, but even with me trying to put the game in Seated mode to add height and sliding the Weapon holster Height to as high as it would go- it's just below my waist. Hardly an issue for me though, but anyone even shorter may be forced to try playing Seated.
I also feel the ammo info counter around your waist is too close, making ne have to put my chin ti my chest to look at it (possibly just my messed uo eyesight?).
Outside of my right hand being stuck in the pointing finger position with no way of fixing it just now (will not be pointing after reloadng the game hopefully), and possibly the fact that there were a small few times when usi g the knife to stab zombies heads, it didn't work. They acted like I punched them then immediately damaged me- I can't say I ran into any noticable bugs or other oddities as of yet, which is always good.

I DEFINITELY recommend evrryone who's ready or the next great zombie shooter and/or survical game to buy this one up now! It's truly the gane we've all been waiting for on Quest ever since finishing Arizona Sunshine 2!

I enjoyed this game. I just beat it today and going  for round two. I'm very picky with zombie games, and I wasn't disappointed with it. You should consider putting grenades in the game. Anyway I recommend this game. Saw some comments about improvements but they have made alot since then. What doesn't need tweaking here and there in the gaming world? Wasn't sure to play this game at first but went ahead and I liked it. Five star

Judged in isolation I would give it 4 stars due to sporadic immersion breaking but not game breaking bugs. 

Judged with respect to the field of VR games? It’s clearly a solid 5 star. Had a few hours of fun for a cheap price. I recommend.

I've had some issues. I usually play seated, picking items up off the ground required me to 1) get out of my chair or 2) change from seated to standing mode, pick up the item, then back to seated mode.(Edit : found the crouch button, much better now)
 I had a real hard time getting past the horde of running zombies that come at you when waiting for the lady to open the door at the top of the stairs. At one point I died and ended up with my waist on the ground. Fixed it by getting on the floor and starting the game. (Edit: seems I may have pressed the crouch button, that I wasnt aware of at the time)
Another time I fell in a ventilation shaft and died. Had to exit the game as there was no "try again" 
So those are the issues I've had.
I like that there are a variety zombie models. So far I've found five different weapons, not a huge amount but sufficient. I got stumped on some of the "puzzles" until I realized your character gives you clues. Others are quite fun to solve.
 I like the weapon upgrade system. I also like the "hidden" items to find. I also like the music and the
I got this first as I love zombie shooters and second to support the small developer team.
Hoping for an update.

Don't get me wrong, it's a good game but in all honesty it was way too easy and the mechanics for aiming the weapons and accuracy of the weapons need to be looked at in great detail becuse you can't hit anything beyond ten feet with anything.

It's an amazing game that is worth every darned penny! I'm not sure how it was so cheap, but I ain't complaining. I am LOVING this game. Not sure why some people are saying it isn't great cause I've played a LOT of the games on here and am a 35 year old who has played video games almost everyday since nintendo and I like it and I'm pretty picky when it comes to games. My check list is
Length of game
Voice acting Story
Secret stuff
Upgradable things, whether it be character or weapons
Graphics
How big the map is
Not in that particular order but what is important and this game has all of it so far.

developer_response.body: Hey there! We're so happy that you are enjoying The Living Remain! The boards behind the house is actually a puzzle, not a bug that needs fixing :) We do not want to post any spoilers here, but if you need some hints/assistance please email us at support@fivefingerstudios.com or DM us on Discord: https://discord.com/invite/g7vYGudHF3

reviews.review_title: Some cool stuff in this game!!
reviews.review_description:
The  electrocution scene is so cool, and the zombie rats!
Amazing! 
Put more stuff like that in your games

The game is really phenomenal considering it was build by a 2-person team.
Story is great, gun mechanics are great, upgrade mechanic is great, price is great.
Graphics feel dated, but it runs smooth on my Quest 2, so I don't complain.

What I really do miss, is a teleport option.
Even after 5+ years in VR, I still get motion sick.
If it ever gets added, let me know and I'll change my rating to 5 stars.

1 suggestion: Add a laser pointer as a possible upgrade for every gun
